info.title: Gallery API
info.description:
Internal HTTP API for the self-hosted media gallery — a **hybrid REST-RPC**
interface served under the `/api` base path. All responses are JSON.

**Conventions**
- Reads return `200` with the resource or collection.
- Creates return `201` with the created resource.
- Updates (`PUT`/`PATCH`) return `200` with the updated resource.
- Deletes return `204` with no body.
- Non-CRUD actions (login, migrate, scan, bulk operations) are `POST`
  verbs that return `200` with a small result object.
- Errors return `{ "error": "<MachineCode>", "message": "<human text>" }`
  with a `4xx`/`5xx` status. `error` is a stable PascalCase code safe to
  branch on; `message` is for display and may change.

**Auth** — a Bearer token (`Authorization: Bearer <token>`) obtained from
`POST /auth/login` (valid 24h). All `GET`s are public; state-changing
methods require a token, except a small public allowlist (media tagging
and the batched `POST /media/by-ids` read).

post.description:
POST /auth/login — Exchange the admin password for a bearer token.
Body: { password }. Returns { token } valid for 24 hours.

POST /danbooru/category-mappings — Map a Danbooru category to a gallery
category. Body: { danbooru_category_id, danbooru_category_name, gallery_category_id }.

POST /danbooru/tag-mappings — Map a Danbooru tag name to a gallery tag name.
Body: { danbooru_tag, gallery_tag }.

put.description:
PUT /danbooru/tag-mappings/{id} — Update a tag-name mapping.
Body: { danbooru_tag, gallery_tag }.

get.description:
GET /duplicates/report — The latest duplicate-scan report, with dismissed
pairs filtered out and each pair enriched with media file names/hashes.

post.description:
POST /duplicates/scan — Run the perceptual-hash duplicate scanner and
return summary stats (images compared, candidates, duplicates found).

POST /duplicates/dismissals — Dismiss a pair of media items as not-duplicates.
Body: { media_id_1, media_id_2 }.

get.description:
GET /media/page/{page}[/{per_page}] — A page of media items.
Returns { items, total_pages, current_page }. per_page is clamped to 1..200.
GET /media/untagged/{page}[/{per_page}] — A page of media with no tags.

get.summary: A page of media filtered by tags
get.description:
GET /media/with-tags/{tag_list}/{page}[/{per_page}] — A page of media
filtered by a comma-separated tag list; a leading '-' excludes a tag
(e.g. "cat,-dog"). Returns { items, total_pages, current_page }.

parameters.name: tag_list
parameters.description: Comma-separated tag names; a leading '-' excludes (e.g. cat,-dog).

get.description: GET /media/{media_id} — A single media item by ID.

delete.description: DELETE /media/{media_id} — Delete a media item (DB row, file, thumbnails).

get.description: GET /media/random — A single random media item.
GET /media/count — The total media count, as { count }.

post.description:
POST /media/by-ids — Get multiple media items by an array of IDs.
Body: { ids: int[] } (max 200). Missing IDs are silently skipped.
A public batched read (uses POST only to carry a large id list).

POST /media/bulk-delete — Delete one or more media items by ID.
Body: { media_ids: int[] }. Returns the deleted and failed ID lists.

get.description: GET /media/{media_id}/tags — Tags applied to a media item. Cached.

patch.summary: Add tags to a media item
patch.description:
PATCH /media/{media_id}/tags — Apply tags to a media item (implications
resolved). Body: { tag_ids: int[] }. Returns the item's updated tag list.
Public (anyone may tag).

delete.summary: Remove a tag from a media item
delete.description:
DELETE /media/{media_id}/tags/{tag_id} — Remove a tag from a media item.
Returns the item's updated tag list. Public (anyone may tag).

post.summary: Import Danbooru tags for a media item

get.description: GET /version — The running application and API contract versions.

GET /openapi.json — The generated OpenAPI 3.1 document.

Serves the spec committed to the repo (regenerated with `composer docs`),
so production does not depend on swagger-php being installed.
GET /docs — The interactive API reference (Scalar), pointed at the spec.

post.description:
POST /tag-categories — Create a tag category. Returns the created category.
Body: { category_name, category_short, color?, description?, sort_order? }

put.description:
PUT /tag-categories/{category_id} — Update a tag category.
Returns the updated category. Body: same fields as create.

delete.description: DELETE /tag-categories/{category_id} — Delete a category that has no tags.

get.description: GET /tags — All tags (id, name, category). Cached.

post.description:
POST /tags — Create a tag. Body: { tag_name, category_id? (default 1) }.
Returns the created tag.

get.summary: List tags with category and usage counts
get.description:
GET /tags/display — All tags with category and usage/implication counts,
for the tags-management page. Cached.
GET /tags/{tag_id} — A single tag by ID.

put.description:
PUT /tags/{tag_id} — Rename and/or recategorize a tag.
Body: { tag_name, category_id? (default 1) }. Returns the updated tag.

delete.description:
DELETE /tags/{tag_id} — Delete a tag.
DELETE /tags/{tag_id}/migrate-to/{target_tag_id} — migrate this tag's media
to the target first, then delete this tag.

delete.summary: Migrate a tag's media to another tag, then delete it

parameters.name: target_tag_id
parameters.description: Migrate media to this tag before deleting.

post.description:
POST /tags/{tag_id}/migrate — Move all media from this tag to the target,
then delete this tag. Body: { target_tag_id }.

POST /tag-implications — Add "tag_id implies implied_tag_id".
Body: { tag_id, implied_tag_id }. Rejects cycles and self-implication.
Returns the created implication.

POST /media — Upload one or more files (multipart/form-data with files[]).
Media type is auto-detected from each file's extension.

fetch_tags.description: Fetch Danbooru tags for uploaded images.

BulkDeleteResult.description: Outcome of a bulk media delete.

failed.description: IDs that could not be deleted.

DanbooruFetchResult.description: Outcome of a Danbooru tag import.

tags.description: The media item's tags after import.

all_tags.description: The full tag list (new tags may have been created).

DuplicateMatch.description: A duplicate media pair with similarity scores.

generated_at.description: ISO timestamp the report was generated.

MediaPage.description: A paginated page of media items.

target_tag_id.description: The target tag that received the media.

TagListItem.description: A tag with category name and usage counts.

media_count.description: Number of media items with this tag.

implication_count.description: Number of implications originating from this tag.

tags_applied.description: Number of tags applied from Danbooru.

total_tags_applied.description: Total Danbooru tags applied (only when fetch_tags was set).

file_time.description: Unix timestamp the file was ingested.

hash.description: MD5 hash of the file contents.

duration.description: Duration in seconds; 0 for still images.

TagCategory.description: A tag category (color, shortcode, sort order).

category_short.description: Short code (<= 5 chars) used in tag prefixes.

bearerAuth.description: Admin bearer token issued by POST /auth/login (valid 24 hours).

tags.name: Media
tags.description: Media items (images + videos), listing, upload, and tagging.
